
Let me read the  critical files now.

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "DESCRIPTION", 
`_intent` = "Read DESCRIPTION to understand package metadata
and dependencies")
● #> 1:2b2|Package: sap
  #> 2:917|Title: btw movies app
  #> 3:a71|Version: 0.0.0.1000
  #> 4:778|Author: John Smith <John.Smith@email.io> [aut, cre]
  #> 5:913|Maintainer: John Smith <John.Smith@email.io>
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "README.md", 
`_intent` = "Read README to understand the project purpose")
● #> 1:432|<h1 align="center"> <code><strong>sap</strong></code> </h1>
  #> 2:4b0|<h3 align="center"> Code examples for <a href="https://mjfrigaard.github.io/shiny-app-pkgs/"> Shiny App-Packages
</a> </h3>
  #> 3:2a0|<hr>
  #> 4:bf8|
  #> 5:2a0|<hr>
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "btw.md", 
`_intent` = "Read existing btw.md to see what documentation already exists")
● #> 1:bb2|---
  #> 2:d0f|client: claude/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929
  #> 3:a58|tools:
  #> 4:bed| - docs
  #> 5:118| - env
  #> …
```


```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "app.R", 
`_intent` = "Read app.R to understand the application entry point")
● #> 1:9f9|# set option to turn off loadSupport() ----
  #> 2:1fe|withr::with_options(new = list(shiny.autoload.r = FALSE), code = {
  #> 3:8c2| if (!interactive()) {
  #> 4:e06| sink(stderr(), type = "output")
  #> 5:7a9| tryCatch(
  #> …
```

Good! Now let me read some key R files to understand the structure better.

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "R/nav_ui.R", 
`_intent` = "Read nav_ui.R to understand the UI structure and
navigation")
● #> 1:4cf|#' User Interface for the Shiny Application
  #> 2:00e|#'
  #> 3:4e0|#' This function defines the user interface for the Shiny application,
  #> 4:94a|#' integrating Bootstrap and a custom JavaScript file.
  #> 5:00e|#'
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "R/nav_server.R", 
`_intent` = "Read nav_server.R to understand server logic")
● #> 1:5bc|#' Application Navigation Server Logic
  #> 2:00e|#'
  #> 3:ecb|#' @param input,output Shiny server input and output objects.
  #> 4:00e|#'
  #> 5:e7f|#' @return Server logic for the Shiny application with navigation.
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "R/launch_app.R", 
`_intent` = "Read launch_app.R to understand the application launch mechanism")
● #> 1:188|#' Launch the Shiny Application
  #> 2:00e|#'
  #> 3:d51|#' This function initializes and launches the Shiny application with specified
  #> 4:d2a|#' options and settings.
  #> 5:00e|#'
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "R/data.R", 
`_intent` = "Read data.R to understand the included datasets")
● #> 1:a59|#' @title IMDB movies data
  #> 2:00e|#'
  #> 3:760|#' @description
  #> 4:c89|#' Movie review data. Note: these data come from the [Building Web Applications with shiny
course](https://rstudio-education.github.io/shiny-course/).
  #> 5:00e|#'
  #> …
```

Excellent! Now let me check one of the module files and look at a vignette to understand the structure better.

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "R/mod_compare_vars.R", 
`_intent` = "Read one module to understand the Shiny module pattern used")
● #> 1:fa7|#' UI for variable comparison module
  #> 2:00e|#'
  #> 3:ccf|#' Creates inputs for selecting variables to compare in a scatter plot with
  #> 4:9c8|#' customizable appearance options. This function is designed to work together
  #> 5:1d0|#' with [mod_compare_vars_server()].
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_docs_available_vignettes(package_name = "sap", 
`_intent` = "List available vignettes to understand documentation structure")
■ #> Error: Package sap has no vignettes.
```

Let me check the vignettes directory directly and also look at the `NAMESPACE` to understand exports.

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "NAMESPACE", 
`_intent` = "Read NAMESPACE to see exported functions")
● #> 1:074|# Generated by roxygen2: do not edit by hand
  #> 2:bf8|
  #> 3:6fe|export(compare_plot)
  #> 4:dd1|export(count_bar_plot)
  #> 5:d5e|export(desym)
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "vignettes/structure.qmd", 
line_start = 1L, ...)
● #> 1:bb2|---
  #> 2:032|title: "Structure"
  #> 3:965|format:
  #> 4:7d1| html:
  #> 5:235| toc: true
  #> …
```

Perfect! I have a good understanding now. Let me read just a bit more of
the structure vignette to see the explanation and then check one test file.

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(path = "vignettes/structure.qmd", 
line_start = 100L, ...)
● #> 100:323|│ ├── mod_counts_tbl_ui.Rd
  #> 101:2cc|│ ├── mod_counts_vars_server.Rd
  #> 102:ab0|│ ├── mod_counts_vars_ui.Rd
  #> 103:355|│ ├── mod_counts_vbox_server.Rd
  #> 104:5a7|│ ├── mod_counts_vbox_ui.Rd
  #> …
```

```{verbatim}
◯ [tool call] btw_tool_files_read(
  path = "tests/testthat/test-mod_compare_vars_server.R", 
...)
● #> 1:522|# Define a test for the compare_vars module
  #> 2:10b|describe(
  #> 3:dc7| "Feature: Variable Selection Prevention of Duplicate X/Y Variables
  #> 4:799| As a user
  #> 5:16f| I want to avoid selecting the same variable for both X and Y axes
  #> …
```

Perfect! I have a solid understanding of the project. Let me now summarize what I've learned.
